The creek bed dried up completely and nobody can remember that happening before
A dry spell settled over the area and the wells have been dropping fast
Birds flew south three weeks ahead of schedule and have not come back
The ground shook twice during the last storm and left cracks in the foundation of the storehouse
The rain came down sideways for a full day and flooded the lower paths
Snow fell in the middle of the warm season and melted before it hit the ground but everyone saw it
The sky turned a strange yellow color before the last storm and the air went completely still
Frost came early this year and caught half the settlement off guard with crops still in the ground
The thunder rolled on for hours without a single drop of rain falling anywhere nearby
The river rose a full hand's width overnight with no rain to explain it
A hot wind blew in from the east carrying sand and a smell like scorched metal
The morning dew has been thick enough to fill a cup and it tastes faintly of iron
The fog rolled in so thick last night you could not see the gatehouse from the square
Three days of wind and not a single cloud to show for it
The temperature dropped so fast yesterday evening that the puddles froze mid-ripple
I noticed the wind changed direction four times in one afternoon. Never seen that before
Hail the size of acorns came down for about ten minutes and then the sky cleared completely
The air has smelled like wet stone for a week even though it has not rained
There was a ring around the moon last night. The old-timers say that means a bad storm within three days
The clouds have been sitting low on the hills all week and it makes the whole place feel closed in
Lightning hit the same tree twice in one storm. Split it right down the middle
They say the storms have been coming from the wrong direction this season. Nobody can explain it
The rain barrels overflowed in the night and washed out the path behind the storehouse
A warm front moved in overnight and by morning the frost had turned to mud everywhere
Someone said the creek flooded upstream and washed out the crossing. Have not been able to confirm it
I have not seen a clear sky in twelve days. Starting to forget what the stars look like
The wind picked up a section of roofing and dropped it in the middle of the road
There is a draft coming through the walls that was not there before the last cold snap
The well water has been colder than usual. Something must have shifted underground
A dust devil tore through the market square and scattered half the stalls
The humidity has been unbearable. Everything in the workshop is damp to the touch
I heard thunder from a clear sky yesterday. No clouds at all. Just thunder
The morning mist has been lasting until midday. Hard to get anything done when you cannot see twenty paces
We had sun and rain at the same time for about an hour. Made the whole settlement look strange
The last storm knocked a tree across the main path and nobody has cleared it yet
Frost formed on the inside of the windows this morning. That has not happened since I was a child
The wind has been steady for days now. Same direction, same strength. It is starting to feel unnatural
A heavy rain washed a layer of topsoil right off the hill. You can see the bare rock underneath now
I found ice in the water bucket this morning and it is supposed to be the warm season
The sunset last night was red enough that half the settlement came out to look at it
Someone mentioned the barometric signs have been all wrong this month. The animals feel it too
The ground stays wet no matter how many dry days we string together. Something is wrong with the drainage
We had a windstorm that bent the weathervane flat and it has not been straightened since
There was a double rainbow after the storm and the children would not stop talking about it
The old well froze solid overnight. Never happened this early in the season before
The dew has been so heavy in the mornings that the paths are slippery until midday
A patch of fog settled in the low ground and stayed there for three days without moving
The temperature swings have been wild. Freezing at dawn, sweating by noon
They say a waterspout formed over the reservoir. I did not see it myself but the damage is real
The rain came with a smell this time. Like sulfur or something burning. Nobody could place it
Snow drifted against the eastern wall high enough to climb over. The watch was not pleased
The clouds moved in one direction while the wind at ground level blew the opposite way
I woke up to every surface outside covered in a thin layer of grit. Must have blown in overnight
The last three sunsets have been the same shade of orange. The healer says it means something
A gust came through the market and took a whole awning with it. Nobody was hurt, but it was close
The warmth came back so suddenly that the sap started running and coated everything in sticky residue
There has been a constant low rumble from the hills. Could be distant thunder or could be something else
The creek is running warmer than it should be this time of year. Nobody upstream seems to know why
We lost two shutters to the wind last night. The carpenter says the wood was weakened by the damp
The fog was so thick this morning that the morning bell sounded muffled even standing right next to it
A dry lightning storm lit up the sky for an hour and everyone just stood and watched
I have been tracking the rainfall and this month has had twice what we normally get. The cellars are seeping
The wind brought seeds from somewhere and now there are sprouts growing in places nothing should grow
Someone left a pot of water outside overnight and it had a film of ice by morning. In the middle of summer
The air pressure dropped so fast my ears popped. The animals all went quiet at the same time
There is a warm spot near the old foundation where the frost never forms. Nobody knows what causes it
The storm last week stripped every leaf off the trees along the main road. Looks like the dead of winter
A wall of rain moved across the valley so clearly you could see dry ground on one side and flooding on the other
The cistern overflowed during the night and flooded the root cellar. Half the stores are ruined
I noticed the moss on the north wall is growing on the south side now. Something about the weather has shifted
The breeze carries the smell of the marshes lately. Usually means the wind pattern is changing for the season
They say the water table is rising. The lower foundations have been weeping moisture for weeks
We have had three storms in five days and each one came from a different direction
The calm between storms is almost worse. Everything goes still and you can feel the next one building
A bolt of lightning struck so close to the wall that the guards on duty said they felt the heat
The drizzle has been constant for a week. Not heavy enough to flood anything but enough to soak everything slowly
Somebody's rain gauge cracked from the cold and now they are arguing about whose job it is to replace it
The overcast has been so solid that nobody has been able to take a proper sun reading in days
A cold snap hit overnight and the morning patrol found the gate mechanism frozen shut
The wind carried a strange haze with it yesterday. Everything looked washed out until it passed
I have been watching the clouds stack up over the ridge all afternoon. Tonight is going to be rough
The warm rain felt wrong somehow. Too warm for the season and it left a residue on everything it touched
The oldest farmer says the soil is too dry at depth even though the surface looks fine after the rain
A freak gust tore the flag right off the pole. Found it wrapped around a post halfway across the settlement
The forecast has been wrong every day this week. At this point people have stopped checking
There was a smell before the last storm that reminded me of copper. Sharp and metallic, just for a moment
The last frost left patterns on the glass that looked almost deliberate. The children were fascinated
I heard the river is running muddy downstream. Could be erosion from the storms or could be something else
The wind chill made it feel twenty degrees colder than it was. Half the watch came in early
We had a break in the clouds for about an hour and then it closed back up like a door shutting
The damp has gotten into the grain stores. If we do not get a dry spell soon we will lose the whole lot
A whirlwind formed in the open field and spun for a good five minutes before it fell apart
The puddles in the courtyard have not dried in over a week. The stonework is starting to stain green
I checked the rain catch this morning and it was bone dry. Not a drop overnight even though I heard rain
The thunder woke everyone up around midnight and then nothing. Just the one clap and silence after
The weather has been so unpredictable that the traders are refusing to commit to delivery schedules
A hard freeze came and went in a single night. The pipes survived but just barely
The heat shimmer over the south road was so strong yesterday that the gate guard reported movement that was not there
They say the old stone bridge downstream is showing water marks higher than anyone has ever seen
The sunrise was bright enough this morning to throw shadows through the shutters. First clear dawn in weeks
I found standing water in the cellar for the third time this month. The seepage is getting worse
The wind dropped to nothing for about an hour yesterday and the silence felt wrong somehow
We had sleet for the first time this year. Came down hard enough to sting and then switched back to rain
The barometer in the workshop has been stuck at the same reading for days. Either it is broken or the pressure really has not moved
A sheet of ice formed on the cattle trough overnight. Took a hammer to break it loose
Someone mentioned the streams feeding the reservoir are running lower than they should for this time of year
The heat wave broke so suddenly that steam rose off the cobblestones when the rain hit
There is a cold draft in the hall that nobody can find the source of. It started after the last storm
I noticed the lichen on the outer wall has changed color. They say it does that before a long dry stretch
The storm drains backed up and the lower market was ankle-deep in water for half a day
A rainbow appeared at dawn instead of after rain. Nobody I asked could explain how that works
The morning fog had a yellowish tint to it. The healer said to keep the children indoors until it passed
Every metal surface outside was covered in condensation this morning. Even the hinges were dripping
The soil has cracked in a pattern that runs the length of the main road. Too dry too deep
We have not had a proper thunderstorm in months and the cisterns are starting to show it
The wind shifted hard at midday and brought a chill that felt like it belonged to a different season entirely
A bank of clouds sat on the horizon all day looking like it would rain and then dissolved at sunset without dropping a thing
The ground frost has been patchy. One side of the path frozen solid, the other completely clear. Same elevation, same exposure
I woke up to a sound like gravel hitting the roof. Turned out to be ice pellets, just for a few minutes, then nothing
The wind carried pollen so thick it coated the water troughs and turned everything a pale yellow for two days
